title: Mom And Pop Art

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence with Homer encountering some mishap
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase, perfectly integrated with character but routine for the show

jokes.text: But, Marge, I'm not like other men. That's why you buy my pants at that special store!
jokes.setup: Marge hints that Homer should do housework like other men
jokes.punchline: Homer admits he's different but for an embarrassing reason
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that reveals Homer's self-awareness about his oddness in an unexpected way

jokes.text: Oh, you know as soon as I take 'em down, there'll just be more hostages.
jokes.setup: Marge complains about hostage ribbons still on their tree
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted logic about perpetual hostage situations
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Homer's characteristic lazy rationalization

jokes.text: And you could at least get that snake out of the piano. [Middle Eastern music] [Hissing]
jokes.setup: Marge lists household tasks Homer hasn't done
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of snake in piano with exotic music playing
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd visual gag that's mentioned casually, typical Simpsons surrealism

jokes.text: Can you live with that, Marge? Huh? Can ya? - Yup! - Can ya? - [Groans] - [Thuds]
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically guilts Marge about ruining his Saturday
jokes.punchline: Marge's cheerful 'Yup!' deflates his drama, he collapses
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay showing Marge's immunity to Homer's guilt trips

jokes.text: [Ned] God... bless him.!
jokes.setup: Homer's dramatic collapse and groaning
jokes.punchline: Ned's bemused religious response to Homer's theatrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned reaction - trying to be charitable about Homer's behavior

jokes.text: You're Pop? No offense, but even I could kick your ass.
jokes.setup: Homer meets elderly 'Pop' at hardware store
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt assessment of the old man's frailty
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer lack of tact, but fairly standard insult comedy

jokes.text: Hey, get off my case! The only reason I'm working here is 'cause I'm trying to get a date with Mom.
jokes.setup: Pop defends himself from Homer's insult
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's only working to impress 'Mom' (his female coworker)
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion - the elderly employee has romantic motivations

jokes.text: Aisle one, next to the cat poison.
jokes.setup: Homer asks for bolt cutters location
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of cat poison as a store landmark
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly, good economy

jokes.text: You mean the janitor's closet? Ha!
jokes.setup: Seymour talks about their 'love nest'
jokes.punchline: Edna reveals it's just a janitor's closet
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Seymour's romantic pretensions

jokes.text: I want a baby now.!
jokes.setup: Seymour asks what's wrong, Edna's been saying 'Ha!' to everything
jokes.punchline: Sudden emotional outburst about wanting a baby
jokes.explanation: Awkward relationship moment, shows desperation beneath Edna's cynicism

jokes.text: [Titters] A man in an apron! [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Celebrity spokesman Doug Vaccaro appears in apron
jokes.punchline: Homer finds the concept of men in aprons inherently funny
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Homer's juvenile sense of humor

jokes.text: No, no! I'm into chicken! How about wild boar? Or swordfish? Or hippo? Mmm. Hippo.
jokes.setup: Salesman mentions different meats for barbecuing
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiasm escalates to bizarre choices like hippo
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's food obsession with absurd choices

jokes.text: Mmm. Hippo. [Guttural Sigh]
jokes.setup: Homer imagines eating hippo
jokes.punchline: His signature food fantasy noise
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic food fantasy sound - perfectly character-integrated but familiar

jokes.text: Can I burn evidence in it? We can all burn evidence in it.
jokes.setup: Homer asks if Bart wants a barbecue pit
jokes.punchline: Bart asks about burning evidence, Homer cheerfully agrees
jokes.explanation: Dark family bonding moment with criminal implications, very Simpsons

jokes.text: 'Le grille'? What the hell is that?
jokes.setup: Homer forced to use French instructions after ruining English ones
jokes.punchline: Complete bafflement at simple French word for 'grill'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer ignorance, perfect character moment with quotable line

jokes.text: Why must I fail in every attempt at masonry? [Crying]
jokes.setup: Homer's barbecue construction goes horribly wrong
jokes.punchline: Overwrought lament about masonry failures specifically
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic self-pity with oddly specific masonry reference

jokes.text: How's your father's project coming along? I think he's almost done. [Screams] Yeah, he's done.
jokes.setup: Marge asks about Homer's progress
jokes.punchline: Lisa's assessment punctuated by Homer's scream of failure
jokes.explanation: Good timing gag with Lisa's deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Sorry. I didn't get this hammer hat by handing out refunds.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to return destroyed barbecue kit
jokes.punchline: Clerk's pride in his 'hammer hat' as symbol of strict policies
jokes.explanation: Funny minor character detail - the hammer hat as status symbol

jokes.text: It teaches them while they learn.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to pass off destroyed barbecue as kids' toy
jokes.punchline: Completely redundant description of educational value
jokes.explanation: Homer's circular logic when desperate, good character consistency

jokes.text: Now hit the road, Kriss Kringle. But- But- You heard me, Pere Noel.
jokes.setup: Clerk calling Homer Santa Claus to mock him
jokes.punchline: Escalates through multiple Santa names including French
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation of Santa references, shows clerk's education

jokes.text: What you got there? Beanie Baby.
jokes.setup: Worker asks what Homer's dumping
jokes.punchline: Homer's massive barbecue wreckage dismissed as 'Beanie Baby'
jokes.explanation: Good deflation and period reference to Beanie Baby craze

jokes.text: All right! My bumper fell off. Not my fault. Act of God, act of God. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Something goes wrong with Homer's car
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately absolves himself with insurance terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer rationalization with insurance jargon

jokes.text: All right! Just go ahead and sue me! Everybody else does! The average settlement is $68,000.
jokes.setup: Homer breaks down when confronted with evidence
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been sued so often he knows average settlements
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail - Homer's been sued so much he's an expert on settlements

jokes.text: You mean this hunk of junk? This isn't art. It's just a barbecue that pushed me over the edge. Didn't you? Didn't you? You stupid- [Shouting]
jokes.setup: Art dealer wants to display Homer's destroyed barbecue
jokes.punchline: Homer attacks the barbecue debris like it's sentient
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's grudge against inanimate objects

jokes.text: Is everything okay? I got worried when I didn't hear any shots.
jokes.setup: Marge comes to check on situation at door
jokes.punchline: She expects violence so absence of gunfire is concerning
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Springfield's violence level, excellent economy

jokes.text: In high school I was voted most likely to be a mental patient, hillbilly or chimpanzee!
jokes.setup: Art dealer explains outsider art can be by mental patients, hillbillies, or chimps
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly recalls being voted most likely to be all three
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - proud of being voted most likely to be three different types of outsiders

jokes.text: Dad, chew with your mouth closed. You're losing your mystique.
jokes.setup: Homer eating messily at art gallery
jokes.punchline: Lisa worried about Homer's artistic image rather than manners
jokes.explanation: Good Lisa line showing her sophistication and concern for Homer's reputation

jokes.text: You squeal on me, I'll kill you.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions famous artist Jasper Johns loving free food
jokes.punchline: Cut to Jasper Johns threatening someone about squealing
jokes.explanation: Great unexpected cut showing famous artist as criminal, good structure

jokes.text: Hey, I done studied for years on how to get over that junkyard fence. Then I learnt the gate was open.
jokes.setup: Hillbilly defending his art education
jokes.punchline: Years of study to climb fence when gate was unlocked
jokes.explanation: Good ironic punchline about misguided effort, fits hillbilly character

jokes.text: Smithers, I think I'm in love! - Hah! - With this sculpture.
jokes.setup: Burns declares love, Smithers reacts with excitement
jokes.punchline: Burns clarifies he means the sculpture, not Smithers
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection playing on Smithers' crush, good structure

jokes.text: Smithers, years ago I blew the chance to buy Picasso's Guernica for a song. Luckily that song was 'White Christmas' and by hanging on to it, I made billions.
jokes.setup: Burns mentions missing chance to buy famous painting 'for a song'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation - he traded the actual song 'White Christmas' for billions
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with Burns taking idiom literally, shows his wealth and twisted logic

jokes.text: In your face, Jasper Johns! Huh?
jokes.setup: Homer celebrates his first art sale
jokes.punchline: Taunts Jasper Johns who responds with confusion
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Jasper Johns reference, modest payoff

jokes.text: Hey, I've always had an interest in art, dating back to my schoolgirl days when I painted portrait after portrait of Ringo Starr. That's my life you're describing! I think I remember my own life, Marge.
jokes.setup: Homer claims artistic background
jokes.punchline: Describes Marge's childhood but thinks it's his own life
jokes.explanation: Good character confusion moment, shows Homer's self-delusion and memory issues

jokes.text: Well, Mom found out her engagement ring is made of rock candy. [Grunting] Good work, honey. Keep it coming.
jokes.setup: Kids try to make Homer angry for his art
jokes.punchline: Lisa reveals Homer's cheap/fake engagement ring, Homer encourages the insults
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation about Homer's cheapness and his masochistic encouragement

jokes.text: Well, I'm flunking math, and the other day I was a little attracted to Milhouse.
jokes.setup: Lisa helping make Homer angry for his art
jokes.punchline: Reveals disturbing attraction to Bart's friend
jokes.explanation: Unexpected confession from Lisa, disturbing but funny

jokes.text: Eh, to be honest, we are adrift in a sea of decadent luxury and meaningless sex.
jokes.setup: Moe asks how being Eurotrash is working out
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of their privileged problems
jokes.explanation: Good satire of pretentious art world types and their fake problems

jokes.text: So, uh, you guys are Eurotrash, huh? How's that working out for you?
jokes.setup: Moe meets European art collectors
jokes.punchline: Direct, tactless question treating 'Eurotrash' like a career choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe line - blunt, inappropriate directness treating insult as job description

jokes.text: Uh-huh. So, uh, where might this sea be located? Hmm?
jokes.setup: Eurotrash describes metaphorical 'sea of decadent luxury'
jokes.punchline: Moe asks for literal geographic location
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe response - missing metaphor and wanting to join the luxury

jokes.text: Hey, Moe! Can I pay with a drawing? Yeah. Nice try there, twelve step.
jokes.setup: Homer gets to pay with art, regular customer asks same
jokes.punchline: Moe rejects regular customer as 'twelve step' (alcoholic)
jokes.explanation: Good contrast showing special treatment for Homer vs. regular customers

jokes.text: Wow! It's like Marge's dream come true- for me!
jokes.setup: Homer gets offered his own art show
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges it was Marge's dream but celebrates it for himself
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's oblivious selfishness

jokes.text: Frankly, I'd be a little turned on.
jokes.setup: Marge asks how Homer would feel if she entered a belching contest
jokes.punchline: Homer finds the idea sexually appealing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriateness, perfectly in character

jokes.text: I mean, look at Bart.
jokes.setup: Homer explains he's screwed up everything he's done
jokes.punchline: Uses his son Bart as primary evidence
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor with Homer casually citing Bart as his failure

jokes.text: What's going on here? You weirdos love this stuff.
jokes.setup: Homer's new art gets no bids at auction
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused complaint to the art crowd
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's entitlement and confusion

jokes.text: Why don't you call us when you get to kitsch? [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Art dealer explains Homer went from hip to boring
jokes.punchline: Suggests he call when he reaches kitsch level
jokes.explanation: Art world satire about the progression of artistic trends

jokes.text: If we hurry, we can still catch the heroin craze. Mmm!
jokes.setup: Art collectors leave Homer's show
jokes.punchline: They're moving on to heroin as the next artistic trend to follow
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of trendy art world following dangerous fads

jokes.text: Hey, Ray J. Johnson never changed his act, and he's more popular now than he's ever been. Who? You can call him Ray. Or you can call him J. Or you can call him Ray J. But you doesn't have to call him- I'm sick of him already.
jokes.setup: Homer defends not changing by citing Ray J. Johnson
jokes.punchline: Lisa doesn't know who he is, Homer starts the routine, she cuts him off
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor with obscure comedian reference

jokes.text: Matt Groening? What's he doing in a museum? He can barely draw! Ow! Oh, no! I'm being erased!
jokes.setup: Homer sees Matt Groening (show creator) in museum
jokes.punchline: Insults creator's drawing skills, gets erased as punishment
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta humor with creator cameo and self-deprecation about art style

jokes.text: He must be a hundred feet tall.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains Claes Oldenburg's work
jokes.punchline: Homer focuses on the artist's physical size rather than artistic significance
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of art scale, missing the point entirely

jokes.text: It's glorious! The streets are paved with water! You could ride a walrus to work!
jokes.setup: Lisa explains Turner painted Venetian canals
jokes.punchline: Homer's excited misunderstanding of Venice's geography
jokes.explanation: Good Homer enthusiasm with absurd transportation idea

jokes.text: They call it his Angry Jerk Period.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains Picasso's evolution ending with crank letters
jokes.punchline: Art historical period named 'Angry Jerk Period'
jokes.explanation: Good art world satire with amusing period name

jokes.text: I could never think of something like soup or a pencil.
jokes.setup: Homer feels intimidated by great artists' genius
jokes.punchline: Thinks soup and pencils are impossibly creative concepts
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's limited perspective on creativity

jokes.text: Hasta la vista, baby!
jokes.setup: Homer is being attacked by museum statues
jokes.punchline: Classical statue delivers Terminator catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Surreal visual gag mixing classical art with modern movie references

jokes.text: See ya in hell, 'God Bless This House.' So long, 'The Simpsons'! D'oh.!
jokes.setup: Homer stealing doormats for art project
jokes.punchline: Accidentally steals his own family's doormat
jokes.explanation: Good self-referential moment with Homer's typical oversight

jokes.text: That's for the courts to decide, Son.
jokes.setup: Bart asks if Homer's project is art or vandalism
jokes.punchline: Homer deflects responsibility to judicial system
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's avoidance of responsibility

jokes.text: Just like Venice, without the black plague.
jokes.setup: Homer compares his flooded Springfield to Venice
jokes.punchline: Mentions avoiding black plague (wrong time/place)
jokes.explanation: Homer's historical confusion mixing up Venice and plague locations

jokes.text: I just wish Jasper Johns hadn't stolen my boat. Aah! So long, suckers.!
jokes.setup: Moe praising Homer's flooded Springfield
jokes.punchline: Reveals Jasper Johns stole his boat and is escaping
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Jasper Johns as criminal artist

jokes.text: [Scoffs] Looks like heaven's easier to get into than Arizona State.
jokes.setup: Ned sees Homer among the apparently saved in flood
jokes.punchline: Compares heaven's admissions to notoriously easy college
jokes.explanation: Great Ned line combining religious concern with educational snark

jokes.text: Hey, they're working! My feet are soaked, but my cuffs are bone-dry! Everything's comin' up Milhouse!
jokes.setup: Milhouse wearing flood pants in the flood
jokes.punchline: Celebrates partial success with signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - finding silver lining in disaster

jokes.text: Do you think mother would like this hat pin? Oh. Yes. Oh, you've made me the happiest man on Earth!
jokes.setup: Romantic Venice-like setting, Seymour about to propose
jokes.punchline: Asks about hat pin for his mother instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Seymour's mother obsession ruining romantic moment

jokes.text: No. I'm just a nut who couldn't build a barbecue.
jokes.setup: Marge calls Homer a true artist after his success
jokes.punchline: Homer's rare moment of honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Rare Homer moment of genuine humility and self-awareness

jokes.text: I need some inspiration.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge about to kiss on rooftop
jokes.punchline: Homer needs inspiration to kiss his wife
jokes.explanation: Mild setup for romantic moment, not particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: Yoink!
jokes.setup: Homer taking something (likely a romantic gesture)
jokes.punchline: Classic Homer stealing sound effect
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, highly quotable but routine for the show

tmdb_overview: Homer's failed do-it-yourself project gets mistaken for "outsider art." Suddenly in the limelight, Homer must come up with new "works" to impress the art world.
insight: Homer's art obsession drives 63 jokes in 22 minutes—highest density of Season 10.
